schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.short_description: Direct income support for farmers, providing ₹6,000 annually in three installments.
schemes.detailed_description: A central sector scheme with 100% funding from the Government of India. The scheme is meant to provide income support to all landholding farmer families in the country to supplement their financial needs for procuring various inputs related to agriculture and allied activities as well as domestic needs.
key_benefits: Annual financial assistance of ₹6,000 provided to eligible farmer families.
Payment is disbursed in three equal installments of ₹2,000 every four months.
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into the Aadhaar-seeded bank accounts of the beneficiaries.
Transparent identification process through the PM-Kisan portal.
schemes.income_criteria: Excludes Higher Income Groups and Income Tax Payers
other_conditions: The farmer family must have cultivable landholding in their name.
Institutional landholders are excluded.
Farmer families holding constitutional posts or retired officers are excluded.
Professionals like Doctors, Engineers, Lawyers, and Chartered Accountants are excluded.
application_steps_online: Go to the official PM-Kisan website and click on 'New Farmer Registration'.
Select Rural or Urban Farmer Registration and enter your Aadhaar number and mobile number.
Select your state and enter the captcha code to receive an OTP.
Verify the OTP and fill in the detailed registration form including land details and bank info.
Upload scanned copies of required land documents and submit.
Take a printout of the registration for future reference.
application_steps_offline: Visit the local Revenue Office (Patwari) or the Nodal Officer designated by the State Government.
Obtain the PM-Kisan registration form and fill it manually.
Attach self-attested copies of land records, Aadhaar, and bank passbook.
Submit the form to the officer for verification and inclusion in the beneficiary database.

submission_locations.address: Local CSC outlets across all villages
submission_locations.notes: Assists with e-KYC and new application entry for a nominal fee.

schemes.validity: As long as landholding status remains valid

faq.question: What is the definition of a 'Family' under PM-Kisan?
faq.answer: A family consists of a husband, wife, and minor children.

common_rejection_reasons: Incorrect bank account details or IFSC code.
Mismatch between Aadhaar name and Land record name.
tips_for_successful_application: Ensure your mobile number is linked to your Aadhaar card for OTP verification.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Mandatory for identity verification and DBT mapping.
Land ownership records (7/12 extract or equivalent) to prove cultivable land.
To verify Bank Account Number and IFSC Code for fund transfer.

schemes.scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.short_description: Life insurance cover of ₹2 Lakh at an affordable premium of ₹436 per annum.
schemes.detailed_description: PMJJBY is a one-year life insurance scheme, renewable from year to year, offering coverage for death due to any reason. It is available to people in the age group of 18 to 50 years having a savings bank account who give their consent to join and enable auto-debit.
key_benefits: ₹2,00,000 life cover in case of death of the insured due to any reason.
Simple and hassle-free enrollment process via bank.
Low premium cost making it accessible for the poor.
Premium is auto-debited from the bank account.
other_conditions: Must have a savings bank account.
Must give consent for auto-debit of premium.
If a person has multiple bank accounts, they can join the scheme through only one account.
application_steps_online: Log in to your bank's net banking portal.
Navigate to the 'Insurance' or 'Social Security Schemes' section.
Select PMJJBY and fill in the nominee details.
Accept the terms and confirm the auto-debit mandate.
application_steps_offline: Visit the bank branch where you hold a savings account.
Fill the PMJJBY application/consent form.
Submit the form along with a copy of your Aadhaar.
Ensure the account has a sufficient balance for the premium.

submission_locations.notes: The scheme is managed through banks directly.

faq.question: Can I have multiple PMJJBY policies?
faq.answer: No, only one policy per person is allowed across all banks.

tips_for_successful_application: Maintain balance in the last week of May for auto-renewal.
Ensure nominee details are correctly updated.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Primary KYC document linked to the bank account.
To verify bank details for auto-debit.

schemes.short_description: Accidental insurance cover of ₹2 Lakh at a premium of just ₹20 per year.
schemes.detailed_description: PMSBY offers protection against death or disability due to an accident. It is available to people in the age group 18 to 70 years with a bank account. The premium of ₹20 per annum is auto-debited from the account holder’s bank account.
key_benefits: ₹2,00,000 for accidental death or total permanent disability.
₹1,00,000 for partial permanent disability.
Extremely affordable premium (₹20/year).
Coverage for accidents like road mishaps, drowning, or falls.
other_conditions: Must have a savings bank account.
Auto-debit mandate is mandatory.
application_steps_online: Log in to Internet Banking.
Select 'PMSBY' under social security schemes.
Provide nominee details and authorize auto-debit.
application_steps_offline: Visit your bank branch.
Submit the PMSBY consent form.
Carry a copy of your passbook and Aadhaar.

submission_locations.notes: Easy to link during new account opening.

faq.question: Is natural death covered?
faq.answer: No, this scheme only covers death or disability caused specifically by an accident.

tips_for_successful_application: Link your mobile number to get auto-debit alerts.
Notify your family members about the policy for claim purposes.

schemes.short_description: Loans between ₹10 Lakh to ₹1 Crore to SC/ST and Women entrepreneurs.
schemes.detailed_description: The objective of the Stand-Up India scheme is to facilitate bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
key_benefits: Substantial funding for starting new businesses.
Refinance window through SIDBI.
Handholding support through a network of agencies.
Lower interest rates for targeted groups.
schemes.category_criteria: SC/ST and Women of all categories
other_conditions: In case of non-individual enterprises, at least 51% of the shareholding and controlling stake should be held by either an SC/ST or Women entrepreneur.
Borrower should not be in default to any bank/financial institution.
application_steps_online: Register on the Stand-Up Mitra portal.
Fill the application form and upload the project report.
The portal will match you with potential lenders.
Track the status of your loan application online.
application_steps_offline: Visit any Scheduled Commercial Bank branch.
Ask for the Stand-Up India loan application.
Submit the project proposal and KYC documents to the branch manager.

submission_locations.notes: Focus on Greenfield (new) projects only.

faq.question: What is a Greenfield project?
faq.answer: A project where the entrepreneur is setting up the business for the first time in the manufacturing, services, or trading sector.

common_rejection_reasons: Proposed business is not a greenfield project.
Poor credit history or prior defaults.
tips_for_successful_application: Prepare a robust project report with financial projections.
Utilize the handholding support provided by SIDBI for technical guidance.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Detailed business plan for the greenfield project.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
schemes.short_description: Provides 5kg of free foodgrains per month to eligible beneficiaries.
schemes.detailed_description: PM-GKAY is a food security welfare scheme. It aims to provide free foodgrains to approximately 80 crore beneficiaries under the National Food Security Act (NFSA). Each beneficiary is entitled to 5kg of free rice or wheat per month.
key_benefits: 5kg free foodgrains per person per month.
Available over and above the regular subsidized NFSA ration.
Prevents hunger and ensures nutritional security for the poor.
Operates through the existing Fair Price Shops (FPS).
other_conditions: Must hold a valid NFSA Ration Card.
Ration card must be linked with Aadhaar.
application_steps_offline: Visit your assigned Fair Price Shop (Ration Shop).
Present your Ration Card and perform biometric (Aadhaar) authentication.
Collect your free 5kg foodgrains along with your regular ration.

submission_locations.who_should_visit: Family member listed on Ration Card
submission_locations.notes: Beneficiaries can use 'One Nation One Ration Card' to collect from any shop.

faq.question: Can I get cash instead of foodgrains?
faq.answer: No, the benefit is only provided in the form of foodgrains (Rice/Wheat).

common_rejection_reasons: Ration card not listed under NFSA.
Biometric mismatch at the e-POS machine.
tips_for_successful_application: Ensure your mobile number is updated for ration alerts.
Verify your weight at the shop before signing/authenticating.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: For biometric authentication at the ration shop.

schemes.scheme_name: PM Schools for Rising India (PM SHRI)
schemes.short_description: Developing 14,500 schools into 'Green Schools' with modern pedagogy.
schemes.detailed_description: PM SHRI Schools will provide high-quality education in an equitable, inclusive, and joyful school environment. These schools will be developed as 'Green Schools', incorporating solar panels, LED lights, nutrition gardens, and waste management.
key_benefits: Smart classrooms and digital libraries.
Vocational training and skill-based learning.
Upgraded sports and lab facilities.
Mentorship for nearby schools.
Promotion of NEP 2020 values.
other_conditions: Student must be enrolled in a designated PM SHRI school.
School selection is based on a challenge method among existing Govt schools.
application_steps_offline: Seek admission in a school designated as 'PM SHRI'.
Standard admission procedures of the respective school apply.

submission_locations.notes: Check the PM SHRI portal to see if your local school is selected.

faq.question: Is it a new school or existing one?
faq.answer: It is an upgrade of existing Central/State/Local body schools.

common_rejection_reasons: Admission criteria of the specific school not met.
tips_for_successful_application: Look for the PM SHRI logo at govt schools in your district.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.short_description: Banking services using Aadhaar authentication at Business Correspondent points.
schemes.detailed_description: AePS allows people to perform basic banking transactions (cash withdrawal, deposit, balance inquiry) at retail outlets using only their Aadhaar number and fingerprint. It promotes financial inclusion in areas without bank branches.
key_benefits: No need to visit a bank branch or ATM.
Safe and secure biometric-based transactions.
Available at local shops (Business Correspondents).
Encourages digital payments in rural India.
other_conditions: The user's bank account must be linked with their Aadhaar number.
The bank must be 'live' on the AePS platform.
application_steps_offline: Visit a local Business Correspondent (BC) or a shop displaying the AePS logo.
Provide your Aadhaar number and the name of your bank.
Select the transaction type (Withdrawal/Inquiry).
Place your finger on the biometric scanner.
Collect the cash or receipt.

submission_locations.address: Local Kirana shops or CSC centers
submission_locations.notes: Verify the agent's identity before sharing biometric info.

faq.question: Are there charges for AePS?
faq.answer: Generally, basic services are free, though some banks/agents may have nominal withdrawal fees.

common_rejection_reasons: Aadhaar not linked to bank account.
tips_for_successful_application: Ensure your Aadhaar is correctly seeded at your home bank branch.
Keep your fingers clean for the biometric scanner.
target_beneficiaries: Elderly who cannot travel to banks

schemes.short_description: Financial incentive for construction of Individual Household Latrines (IHHL).
schemes.detailed_description: SBM-G aims to achieve an Open Defecation Free (ODF) India. The scheme provides a financial incentive of ₹12,000 to eligible rural households for constructing and using a toilet.
key_benefits: ₹12,000 incentive for toilet construction.
Promotes health and hygiene in rural areas.
Dignity and safety for women and children.
Technical guidance for twin-pit technology.
schemes.income_criteria: BPL and specified APL categories (SC/ST, Small Farmers)
other_conditions: Household must not have an existing toilet.
Beneficiary must not have received benefits for a toilet earlier.
application_steps_online: Register on the SBM-G portal.
Fill the application form for IHHL incentive.
Upload basic KYC documents.
Upload photos of the completed toilet to claim the funds.
application_steps_offline: Apply at the local Gram Panchayat office.
Submit documents to the Gram Sevak.
Physical verification by the block official.

submission_locations.notes: Community toilets are also managed under this scheme.

faq.question: Can I get money before building the toilet?
faq.answer: Generally, the payment is made after construction or in stages based on geotagged photo verification.

common_rejection_reasons: Existing functional toilet in the house.
tips_for_successful_application: Follow the twin-pit model recommended for rural waste management.
Ensure the name on the bank account matches Aadhaar.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.short_description: Promotes micro-irrigation (Drip/Sprinkler) to save water and improve yields.
schemes.detailed_description: PMKSY (Per Drop More Crop) focuses on water use efficiency at the farm level. It provides subsidies to farmers for installing modern micro-irrigation systems, reducing water wastage.
key_benefits: Subsidies on Drip and Sprinkler irrigation kits.
Up to 55% subsidy for small/marginal farmers.
Up to 45% subsidy for other farmers.
Significant reduction in fertilizer and labor costs.
Increased crop productivity.
other_conditions: Must have a source of water (Well/Borewell).
Soil/Water testing may be required.
application_steps_online: Visit the state's agriculture portal (e.g., Mahadbt for Maharashtra).
Select 'Irrigation Schemes'.
Upload land and bank details.
Choose an authorized vendor and submit the quote.
application_steps_offline: Visit the District Agriculture Officer (Taluka Krishi Adhikari).
Submit the physical application form with land records.
Verification by the Krishi Sahayak.

submission_locations.notes: Funds are often limited by annual budget cycles; apply early.

faq.question: Can I install it myself?
faq.answer: To get the subsidy, installation must be done by a government-empanelled vendor.

common_rejection_reasons: Land records not in applicant's name.
Previous micro-irrigation subsidy availed within last 7-10 years.
tips_for_successful_application: Perform a soil test before selecting the type of system.
Keep your electricity bill of the farm pump ready.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Price estimate of the irrigation system.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.short_description: Provides timely credit to farmers for agricultural and allied activities.
schemes.detailed_description: The KCC scheme aims at providing adequate and timely credit support from the banking system to the farmers for their cultivation and other needs (Post-harvest expenses, produce marketing, consumption requirements). It is now extended to Animal Husbandry and Fisheries.
key_benefits: Low interest rate (approx 4% after interest subvention).
Collateral-free loans up to ₹1.6 lakh.
Flexible repayment based on harvest cycles.
Atm-enabled debit card (KCC Card) for easy withdrawal.
Includes crop insurance cover.
schemes.financial_assistance: Loan based on land and crop type
other_conditions: Owner cultivators and tenant farmers are eligible.
Self Help Groups (SHGs) or Joint Liability Groups (JLGs) of farmers are eligible.
schemes.online_apply_link: Via PM-Kisan portal or bank apps
application_steps_online: Download KCC form from PM-Kisan portal.
Submit digitally if your bank allows, or print and take to the bank.
Existing PM-Kisan beneficiaries have a simplified 1-page form.
application_steps_offline: Visit your nearest commercial/co-operative bank.
Fill the KCC application form.
Submit land records and ID proof.
Bank official will visit the farm for verification.

submission_locations.notes: Co-operative banks are very active in KCC distribution.

faq.question: Can I use KCC for personal needs?
faq.answer: A portion of the KCC limit (usually 10%) can be used for household consumption needs.

tips_for_successful_application: Repay within one year to get the 3% interest subvention benefit.
Apply at the bank where you receive PM-Kisan installments.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: From other banks in the area.

schemes.short_description: Grant of ₹15,000 to purchase modern tools for traditional artisans.
schemes.detailed_description: A part of the PM Vishwakarma scheme, this grant is provided after the completion of 'Basic Skill Training'. It helps artisans upgrade from traditional to modern, efficient tools.
key_benefits: ₹15,000 e-voucher or grant for toolkits.
Reduces physical labor and improves finish quality.
Enables artisans to take on larger, modern orders.
Zero repayment required for this amount (as it is a grant).
other_conditions: Must have completed 5-7 days of Basic Training under PM Vishwakarma.
Must be a registered Vishwakarma artisan.
application_steps_offline: Complete the 5-7 day basic training session.
The incentive is credited or an e-voucher is issued automatically post-certification.
Redeem the voucher at authorized tool suppliers.

submission_locations.notes: Verify the list of tools allowed under your specific trade.

faq.question: Can I get cash instead of tools?
faq.answer: The benefit is provided as an e-voucher/grant specifically meant for buying tools related to the registered trade.

tips_for_successful_application: Ensure your biometric attendance is recorded daily during training.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
schemes.short_description: Financial support to organizations for engaging apprentices and providing stipends.
schemes.detailed_description: NAPS aims to promote apprenticeship training and increase the engagement of apprentices by sharing the cost of stipend with employers. This provides youth with hands-on industry experience.
key_benefits: Monthly stipend for apprentices.
Sharing of 25% of prescribed stipend (up to ₹1,500/month) by Govt.
Cost of basic training sharing for apprentices without formal training.
Nationally recognized experience certificate.
other_conditions: Must be registered on the Apprenticeship portal.
Minimum qualification: 5th standard (depends on trade).
application_steps_online: Register as a 'Candidate' on the portal.
Complete your profile and upload documents.
Search for apprenticeship opportunities in your city.
Apply to companies and attend interviews.

faq.question: Is a job guaranteed after apprenticeship?
faq.answer: While not guaranteed, many companies absorb their apprentices as full-time employees.

tips_for_successful_application: Keep your profile 100% complete on the portal for better visibility to employers.

schemes.short_description: Official registration for small businesses to avail government benefits.
schemes.detailed_description: Udyam Registration is the new process of MSME registration. Any person who intends to establish a micro, small or medium enterprise may file Udyam Registration online in the Udyam Registration portal.
key_benefits: Collateral-free loans from banks.
Eligibility for Mudra loans.
Protection against delayed payments from buyers.
Subsidies on Patent registration and ISO certification.
Preference in Government tenders.
other_conditions: Must have an Aadhaar and PAN.
Investment and Turnover must be within MSME limits.
application_steps_online: Visit the Udyam portal and enter Aadhaar number.
Validate with OTP.
Fill in business name, address, and bank details.
Enter investment and turnover figures.
Submit and download the Udyam Certificate instantly.

submission_locations.notes: Registration is free; do not pay middlemen.

faq.question: Is registration mandatory?
faq.answer: It is mandatory to avail any MSME-specific benefits or subsidies from the Govt.

tips_for_successful_application: Ensure your GST and PAN data are updated as the portal fetches data from those databases.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Required only for certain turnover limits.

schemes.short_description: Cash incentive for institutional delivery (hospital birth) to reduce maternal mortality.
schemes.detailed_description: JSY is a safe motherhood intervention under the NHM. It provides cash assistance with the objective of reducing maternal and neonatal mortality by promoting institutional delivery among poor pregnant women.
key_benefits: Cash assistance of ₹700 to ₹1,400 (varies by rural/urban and state).
Free transport to hospital through 102/108 ambulance.
Asha worker incentive for escorting the mother.
Free drugs and consumables during delivery.
other_conditions: Delivery must take place in a Government or empanelled private health facility.
Limited to first two live births (varies in some states).
application_steps_offline: Register pregnancy at the local PHC/Sub-center.
Contact the local ASHA worker.
Opt for delivery at a government hospital.
Collect the cash incentive before discharge or via DBT post-delivery.

submission_locations.notes: ASHA workers are the primary facilitators for this scheme.

faq.question: Is this separate from PMMVY?
faq.answer: Yes, JSY is for delivery costs, while PMMVY is for wage loss and nutrition support.

common_rejection_reasons: Delivery at a non-empanelled private hospital.
tips_for_successful_application: Keep your MCP card updated with at least 3 Antenatal checkups.

schemes.short_description: Financial assistance to destitute, blind, disabled, and elderly persons in Maharashtra.
schemes.detailed_description: This scheme provides a monthly pension to individuals who are destitute, disabled (above 40%), or suffering from major illnesses. It targets those with no source of income and no family to support them.
key_benefits: Monthly pension of ₹1,000 to ₹1,500 (depends on family members).
Financial security for those unable to work.
Coverage for widows and divorced women.
Coverage for patients of TB, Cancer, HIV, etc.
other_conditions: Must be a resident of Maharashtra for at least 15 years.
Must not have children above 18 years of age who can support them (exceptions for disabled children).
application_steps_online: Register on Aaple Sarkar portal.
Select Social Justice Department.
Upload required documents and fill the pension form.
Track status through the portal.
application_steps_offline: Visit the Collector/Tahsildar office.
Obtain the form for Sanjay Gandhi Niradhar Yojana.
Submit with self-attested documents.
Verification by the Gram Sevak/Talathi.

submission_locations.notes: Decision is usually taken by a committee headed by the Tahsildar.

faq.question: What if I turn 65?
faq.answer: You may be moved to the Shravanbal Seva Rajya Nivruttivetan Yojana (Senior Citizen Pension).

common_rejection_reasons: Having adult sons who can provide support.
tips_for_successful_application: Ensure your disability certificate is from a Government Civil Hospital and states at least 40% disability.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Birth certificate or school leaving certificate.
If applying under disability category (Civil Surgeon issued).

schemes.short_description: Monthly pension for senior citizens above 65 years in Maharashtra.
schemes.detailed_description: This is a state-level pension scheme for senior citizens. It provides financial assistance to those who are 65 years and older and have very low family income. It is divided into Category A (for those on the BPL list) and Category B (for non-BPL with low income).
key_benefits: Monthly pension of ₹1,000 to ₹1,500.
Combined with Central Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension for higher benefit.
Ensures dignity in old age.
Direct credit to bank account.
other_conditions: Must be a resident of Maharashtra for at least 15 years.
Name should ideally be in the BPL list for faster approval.
application_steps_online: Register on Aaple Sarkar.
Choose Shravanbal Seva Yojana.
Upload scanned documents.
Submit and track Assessment ID.
application_steps_offline: Visit the Tehsil office or Collectorate.
Fill the physical application form.
Submit documents and get an acknowledgment receipt.

submission_locations.notes: Agents at CSC centers can help with the online process.

faq.question: Can both husband and wife apply?
faq.answer: Yes, if both meet the age and individual/family income criteria.

tips_for_successful_application: Verify your name in the BPL list (2002/SECC 2011) to simplify the process.

schemes.scheme_name: Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Shulk Prati-Purti Yojana
schemes.short_description: Fee waiver (EBC scholarship) for students from economically weaker sections.
schemes.detailed_description: This scheme provides 50% reimbursement of tuition fees and exam fees to students of EBC (Economically Backward Class) categories pursuing professional or non-professional courses in Maharashtra.
key_benefits: 50% fee reimbursement for various courses.
Applicable for Engineering, Medical, MBA, Arts, Commerce, Science.
Reduces the financial burden of higher education on parents.
Direct Benefit Transfer through MahaDBT portal.
other_conditions: Admission must be through the Centralized Admission Process (CAP).
Student must be a domicile of Maharashtra.
Only two children of the family can avail this benefit.
application_steps_online: Create profile on MahaDBT portal.
Upload Aadhaar, Income, and Domicile details.
Select 'Directorate of Higher Education'.
Apply for Rajarshi Shahu Maharaj Fee Reimbursement.
College clerk verifies and forwards to the department.

faq.question: Does it cover private colleges?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers private unaided and aided colleges if the admission is through CAP.

common_rejection_reasons: Caste category student applying (they have separate schemes).
tips_for_successful_application: Apply immediately after taking admission to ensure early processing.
Ensure your Aadhaar is linked to the bank account for NPCI mapping.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Proof of admission through official channel.

schemes.scheme_name: Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana (MJPJAY)
schemes.short_description: Cashless health insurance for Maharashtra citizens up to ₹5 Lakh.
schemes.detailed_description: MJPJAY (formerly RGJAY) is a flagship health insurance scheme of the Maharashtra Government. It provides cashless treatment for 996 medical procedures (surgeries, therapies) in empanelled hospitals for families holding Yellow, Orange, or Antyodaya ration cards.
key_benefits: Cashless medical treatment up to ₹5,00,000 per family per year.
Coverage for surgeries like cardiac, oncology, neurosurgery, etc.
Free consultation, medicines, and follow-up for 10 days post-discharge.
No waiting period for pre-existing diseases.
Combined with PMJAY for seamless coverage.
schemes.income_criteria: Holders of Yellow, Orange, or Antyodaya Ration Cards
other_conditions: Must be a resident of Maharashtra.
Treatment must be taken in an empanelled hospital (public or private).
application_steps_offline: Visit an empanelled hospital.
Contact the 'Arogyamitra' at the MJPJAY helpdesk.
Show your Ration Card and Aadhaar for verification.
The hospital will send an online pre-authorization request to the insurance company.
Once approved, the surgery/treatment is done cashlessly.

submission_locations.notes: Look for the MJPJAY logo at the entrance of hospitals.

faq.question: Is diagnostic cost covered?
faq.answer: Diagnostic costs are covered only if they lead to an empanelled procedure/surgery.

common_rejection_reasons: Procedure not in the list of 996 surgeries.
White ration card holder (unless specific districts).
tips_for_successful_application: Always check the empanelled hospital list on the website before admission.
ASHA workers can help identify patients for this scheme through village health camps.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: From a government doctor or MJPJAY health camp.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
schemes.short_description: Subsidies for installing solar panels on residential buildings.
schemes.detailed_description: The scheme encourages households to install solar panels on their rooftops. It provides a significant subsidy on the installation cost and allows users to sell excess electricity back to the grid (Net Metering).
key_benefits: Up to 40% subsidy for systems up to 3kW.
Up to 20% subsidy for systems between 3kW to 10kW.
Reduced electricity bills.
Income from selling surplus power.
Eco-friendly energy source.
other_conditions: Must have suitable roof space.
Installation must be done by MNRE authorized vendors.
application_steps_online: Register on the National Portal for Solar Rooftop.
Enter Electricity consumer number and distribution company (DISCOM).
Select the vendor and capacity.
Install and get inspected by DISCOM.
Subsidy is credited after net-metering installation.

submission_locations.notes: Consult with an empanelled vendor; they handle most of the paperwork.

faq.question: Does it work at night?
faq.answer: Without batteries, it doesn't work at night; however, you save money during the day through net-metering.

tips_for_successful_application: Check your shadow-free roof area (approx 100 sq.ft per 1kW).

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Latest bill in the name of the applicant.

schemes.short_description: Incentives for street vendors to adopt digital transactions.
schemes.detailed_description: As part of the PM SVANidhi scheme, vendors who use digital payment modes (like QR codes) for their sales and purchases are eligible for monthly cashback incentives up to ₹100.
key_benefits: Cashback of ₹50 for first 50 digital transactions.
Next 50 transactions: ₹25 cashback.
Next 100 transactions: ₹25 cashback.
Builds a credit profile for higher loan tranches (₹20k and ₹50k).
other_conditions: Must have an active SVANidhi loan account.
Must use BHIM, PhonePe, Google Pay, or other UPI apps.
application_steps_online: Onboard as a digital merchant using any UPI app.
Perform at least ₹25 per transaction.
Cashback is credited automatically to the bank account next month.

faq.question: What is the minimum transaction value?
faq.answer: Each transaction should be at least ₹25.

tips_for_successful_application: Encourage customers to pay via QR code even for small amounts.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.short_description: Pension scheme for small and marginal farmers providing ₹3,000 monthly pension after 60 years of age.
schemes.detailed_description: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maandhan Yojana (PM-KMY) is a voluntary and contributory pension scheme for small and marginal farmers. Under this scheme, farmers between 18 to 40 years of age can enroll and contribute monthly amounts ranging from ₹55 to ₹200 based on their entry age. Upon reaching 60 years, they receive a fixed pension of ₹3,000 per month. In case of death of the beneficiary, the spouse is entitled to receive 50% of the pension as family pension.

schemes.short_description: Business loans from ₹5 Lakh to ₹10 Lakh for established micro-enterprises without collateral.
schemes.detailed_description: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY) provides loans to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises under three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishor (₹50,001 to ₹5 Lakh), and Tarun (₹5 Lakh to ₹10 Lakh). The Tarun category is designed for well-established micro-enterprises that need growth capital. These loans are provided by banks, NBFCs, and MFIs without requiring collateral, making it easier for entrepreneurs to expand their businesses.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
schemes.short_description: World's largest health insurance scheme providing ₹5 Lakh annual coverage per family for secondary and tertiary hospitalization.
schemes.detailed_description: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) is the world's largest health insurance/ assurance scheme fully financed by the government. It provides health cover of ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization. The scheme covers over 10.74 crore poor and vulnerable families (approximately 50 crore beneficiaries) providing coverage for almost 40% of India's population. The scheme is cashless and paperless at empanelled hospitals.
